sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Info action proposing discussion of reforms to Cardano treasury governance, including strategic budgeting, dedicated entities, expert commissions, and budget structure guardrails.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Introduce basic ideas for reforming treasury governance into public discussion, Outline guardrails for strategic direction, Encourage ecosystem collaboration on refining treasury governance processes, Propose a sequence of votes on NCL, budget, and overall treasury withdrawal, Propose thematic budget categories including protocol infrastructure, tooling, research, commercial growth, administration, community, other public-good initiatives, and functional reserve

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state with a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the governance system.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal claims current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and ecosystem-harming conflict.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal argues treasury governance should provide clear direction, coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano's growth and stability.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal claims the NCL should not be treated as an overall budget and is only a vague initial approximation for budgeting.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal recommends a dedicated investment budget that includes strategic direction and a roadmap.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal recommends that DReps vote on balanced budgets rather than isolated individual project withdrawals competing against one another.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal states that Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state with a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the governance system.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and ecosystem-harming conflict.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims the NCL should not be treated as an overall budget and is only a vague initial approximation for budgeting.

Independent evidence for: The proposal claims direct comparison of individual proposals is cognitively inefficient because few DReps are experts in one domain and hardly any are competent across multiple domains.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal argues treasury governance should provide clear direction, coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano's growth and stability." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states that Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state with a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the governance system." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
